title: elymus caput-medusae subsp. bobartii
intro: Elymus caput-medusae subsp. bobartii, also known as the Korean wild rye, is a subspecies of grass native to Korea and parts of Asia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Elymus caput-medusae subsp. bobartii include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, it is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be consumed to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
